founders.name: Michael Rodgers
founders.background: AI engineer and multi-agent systems architect with expertise in building intelligent, autonomous software systems. Likely experienced in LLM orchestration, reinforcement learning, and scalable backend architecture relevant to adaptive fitness personalization.

product_description: TransformFit is an AI-powered adaptive fitness platform designed to deliver hyper-personalized coaching experiences through intelligent workout generation, real-time adaptation, and behavioral retention systems. The platform leverages multi-agent AI architecture to tailor training programs to individual user goals, fitness levels, and progress patterns. Key capabilities include dynamic workout programming that evolves with the user, AI coaching agents that provide contextual guidance and motivation, and behavioral economics-informed engagement loops designed to reduce dropout rates and sustain long-term habit formation. The platform targets the critical gap between generic fitness apps and expensive personal trainers by offering scalable, intelligent, and emotionally aware fitness guidance accessible via web and mobile interfaces.
tech_stack: OpenAI API or equivalent LLM provider (inferred for AI coaching layer)
Possibly LangChain or similar agent orchestration framework (inferred from multi-agent background)
target_market: Health-conscious adults aged 25–45 seeking AI-personalized fitness guidance as an affordable alternative to personal trainers. Includes fitness beginners overwhelmed by generic programs, intermediate users experiencing plateaus, and busy professionals who need adaptive, time-efficient workout planning. Secondary segments may include biohackers, self-quantifiers, and users already engaged with wearable fitness tracking devices.
funding_status: Bootstrapped — self-funded with no external venture or angel investment. Operating under a lean budget ceiling of $200/month for AI infrastructure costs.
key_competitors: Fitbod — AI-driven strength training app with adaptive workout generation
Noom — Behavioral psychology-based weight loss and wellness coaching platform
Peloton — Premium connected fitness hardware and content subscription platform
WHOOP — Wearable biometric tracking focused on recovery, strain, and sleep optimization
MyFitnessPal — Nutrition and calorie tracking platform with large user base and social features
current_challenges: 74–77% Day-1 user abandonment — critical onboarding failure rate indicating users are not reaching the core value proposition before disengaging
No persistent agent memory — AI coaching agents lack continuity between sessions, preventing personalized long-term relationship building and contextual coaching
Manual feature prioritization — absence of data-driven or AI-assisted product decision-making, creating bottlenecks and misalignment with user needs

No behavioral economics integration — lack of engagement mechanics such as loss aversion, variable reward schedules, commitment devices, or social accountability loops that drive habit formation and retention
constraints: Solo founder — all product, engineering, design, and business development responsibilities fall on a single individual, limiting execution bandwidth
AI infrastructure budget capped at $200/month — requires cost-efficient LLM usage strategies such as prompt caching, model tiering, batching, and selective inference to remain viable
Single engineer deployment — infrastructure must prioritize simplicity, managed services, and automation to minimize operational overhead and avoid the need for a dedicated DevOps or backend team
